seed_problem: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.
mutation: composite: turn the fixed-set pickup-delivery TSP into a prize-collecting selection problem (goal) over a Manhattan signal-grid metric with per-arrival red-light delays (input), so both WHICH jobs to serve and the precedence-constrained ordering must be optimized jointly; optimum is NP-hard and serve-all vs serve-none are both suboptimal.
title: Signal-Grid Courier: Prize-Collecting Pickup-and-Delivery Tour
strategies: do-nothing baseline: serve no job (cost equals the sum of all penalties)
append-greedy: sort jobs by penalty and tack pickup+delivery onto the end of the route whenever the marginal drive is cheaper than the forfeited penalty
cheapest-insertion: repeatedly insert the most profitable job at its best pickup/delivery positions until no positive-savings job remains
insertion + or-opt local search with randomized restarts: perturb job order across restarts, re-insert and relocate segments, keep the lowest-cost feasible tour
